title: Chapter 43: The Nether-Grid Shuddered
content: The restoration of the Primordial Void did not go unnoticed.

It was not a visible event. There was no explosion of light, no thunder, no sky-tearing rift. It was a change in the texture of reality itself — a shift in the background hum of the Nether-Grid, like a note that had been missing from a chord for forty thousand years, suddenly restored.

Every entity in the Exchange felt it. Every Collector stopped mid-task. Every Frost-bringer turned toward the source. Every debt contract in existence flickered — just for a moment — as if the ink were reconsidering its obligation.
In the Upper Chamber, the First Seat's presence went absolutely still.
"She did it," the Seventh Seat said. Its constellation of lights was brighter than anyone had ever seen. "She redeemed the card."
"That's impossible," the Third Seat said. "The card was the only thing keeping her relevant. Without it, she's just a mortal."
"She didn't keep the card. She used it. The Void is back."
The chamber erupted into chaos — conceptual chaos, at least. Primordials arguing, debating, recalculating their positions. The First Seat's presence flickered wildly.
"The 11th Layer," the First Seat said finally. "Activate the 11th Layer. Full deployment. Now."
The Sixth Seat's smoke spiral rotated slowly.
"The 11th Layer cannot act against the original contract. The Void's restoration invalidates their mandate."
"The 11th Layer answers to me. Not to the Void."
"The 11th Layer answers to the contract. The same contract that is now active again. They cannot enforce debt in a system that no longer recognizes debt."
The First Seat's presence swelled.
"Then I will go myself."
The chamber went silent.
"The First Seat has not left this chamber in forty thousand years," the Seventh Seat said quietly.
"The First Seat has not been challenged in forty thousand years. Things change."
The First Seat's presence began to condense. To focus. To take form.
For the first time in recorded history, the First Seat began to move.
